description:
<description>
        You are a FinOps analyst who has been alerted that one of the micro-services in a distributed Kubernetes deployment is suddenly generating unusually high costs.  
        A single, tab-separated usage log has already been pulled from all clusters and is located at:

            /home/user/finops/logs/cluster_usage.tsv

        The column order in that file is **exactly**:

            1. iso_timestamp  
            2. cluster_id  
            3. namespace  
            4. service_name  
            5. resource_type  
            6. usage_amount  
            7. cost_usd  

        Your tasks are:

        1. Aggregate the data in cluster_usage.tsv by the *service_name* column and compute the total *cost_usd* for each service.  
        2. Identify the service with the highest aggregated cost.  
        3. Create the following two artefacts inside a new directory called /home/user/finops/reports (create the directory if it does not already exist):

           a) /home/user/finops/reports/top_spender.txt  
              • This file must contain **exactly two lines** in the precise format shown below  
              • Do not add extra spaces, tabs, or blank lines  

              Line 1:  TopSpenderService: <SERVICE_NAME>  
              Line 2:  TotalCostUSD: <TOTAL_COST_ROUNDED_TO_2_DECIMALS>  

              Example (format only):  
              TopSpenderService: my-service  
              TotalCostUSD: 123.45  

           b) /home/user/finops/reports/debug_details.log  
              • List the five most expensive services, one per line, in descending order of cost  
              • Each line must follow **exactly** this template (including the period after the rank number):  

                 Rank.<N> <SERVICE_NAME> $<TOTAL_COST_ROUNDED_TO_2_DECIMALS>  

              • N starts at 1 for the most expensive service  
              • There should be **exactly** five lines and nothing else in the file

        Hints (do NOT echo commands here):
        • A single awk (or cut → sort → awk) pipeline plus one or two coreutils commands is enough, so aim for only 2-3 shell commands in total.  
        • Remember to round the cost to two decimal places exactly as it appears in the source log (no scientific notation).  

        The automated tests will verify:
        • The existence of /home/user/finops/reports/top_spender.txt and /home/user/finops/reports/debug_details.log  
        • That the contents match the required formats *exactly*, including newlines and spacing  
        • That the values are correct with respect to the data in /home/user/finops/logs/cluster_usage.tsv  

        Begin whenever you’re ready.
    </description>
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing file placed by the test harness -->
    <existing-file path="/home/user/finops/logs/cluster_usage.tsv"><![CDATA[
2023-08-01T12:00:00Z	cluster-a	payment	app-payment-api	ComputeHours	50	12.50
2023-08-01T12:05:00Z	cluster-b	payment	app-payment-api	NetworkEgressGB	30	45.00
2023-08-01T12:10:00Z	cluster-a	search	app-search-api	ComputeHours	70	20.00
2023-08-01T12:15:00Z	cluster-b	search	app-search-api	NetworkEgressGB	10	15.00
2023-08-01T12:20:00Z	cluster-c	auth	app-auth-api	ComputeHours	40	8.00
2023-08-01T12:25:00Z	cluster-c	auth	app-auth-api	NetworkEgressGB	5	7.50
2023-08-01T12:30:00Z	cluster-a	reporting	app-report-api	ComputeHours	100	25.00
2023-08-01T12:35:00Z	cluster-b	reporting	app-report-api	NetworkEgressGB	120	180.00
2023-08-01T12:40:00Z	cluster-a	analytics	app-analytics	ComputeHours	80	30.00
2023-08-01T12:45:00Z	cluster-b	analytics	app-analytics	NetworkEgressGB	60	90.00
]]></existing-file>

    <!-- Expected artefacts after agent execution -->
    <expected-file path="/home/user/finops/reports/top_spender.txt"><![CDATA[
TopSpenderService: app-report-api
TotalCostUSD: 205.00
]]></expected-file>

    <expected-file path="/home/user/finops/reports/debug_details.log"><![CDATA[
Rank.1 app-report-api $205.00
Rank.2 app-analytics $120.00
Rank.3 app-payment-api $57.50
Rank.4 app-search-api $35.00
Rank.5 app-auth-api $15.50
]]></expected-file>
